page: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, built on the x86_64 architecture. The compute resource is powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, containing 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. This instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. Local storage is bundled with 180 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this configuration.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mixed profile. The AMD EPYC-Rome processor demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance falls into the average category. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 355.85 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are poor, falling into the bottom 10% at 17.9 GB/sec and 12.56 GB/sec respectively. Passmark memory and database operations scores also rank in the bottom 10%, indicating constraints during memory-intensive workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high density of vCPUs and NVMe storage relative to its resource class, making it a budget-friendly option for compute-focused tasks that do not require dedicated CPU cores or massive memory bandwidth. However, the shared CPU allocation and poor large-block memory throughput represent significant tradeoffs. This server is best suited for workloads such as basic web serving, light single-threaded processing, development environments, and entry-level compute tasks where storage speed is prioritized over sustained multi-core memory throughput.
description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 4 shared vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers strong single-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks, though its multi-core capabilities remain average. A key tradeoff is its memory subsystem; while small-block cached reads perform adequately, large-block uncached read and write speeds fall into the bottom 10%. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and shared CPU allocation, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for entry-level compute tasks. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and development environments that benefit from fast local NVMe storage but do not demand high memory bandwidth or dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 180 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance with some memory bandwidth tradeoffs.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining shared AMD EPYC compute cores with fast local NVMe storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
8.0 GB system memory and 180 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Significant memory bandwidth bottlenecks on large-block operations
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
